items.title: Kalshi Seeks Approval for Perpetual Futures Tied to Gold, Silver
items.description: Kalshi Inc. is seeking approval to expand precious-metals trading on its platform with a popular type of derivative that never expires for gold, silver and platinum.

items.title: London’s 24-Hour Trading Is Unlikely to Revive Market, GAM Says
items.description: The London Stock Exchange’s plan to introduce nearly 24-hour trading for equities may fall short of reviving the bourse’s fortunes, the head of GAM Investments’ global equities team said.

items.title: Indian Refiners Halt Iraq Oil Loadings as Hormuz Risks Escalate
items.description: Two Indian state-run refiners have suspended crude oil loadings from Iraq as mounting security risks in the Strait of Hormuz make it increasingly difficult to ensure safety of vessels, according to pe

items.title: Short Sellers Ramp Up Bets Against HelloFresh’s Debut Bond
items.description: Bets against HelloFresh SE’s debut bond, issued only three weeks ago, have surged after its trading got off to a rocky start earlier this month.

items.title: Colombia, Peru Send Early Policy Signs to Extend Election Rally
items.description: Investors are finding fresh reasons to buy Colombian and Peruvian assets as the countries’ newly elected presidents deliver early signals that their market-friendly agendas will translate into policy

items.title: InMobi Appoints JPMorgan, Jefferies for Nearly $1 Billion IPO
items.description: InMobi Pte has appointed four banks for an initial public offering over the next few months to raise about $1 billion for the Indian mobile advertising company.

items.title: Biotech IPOs Outperform AI Listings, Leading US Market in 2026
items.description: The biotechnology sector is stealing the US IPO market thunder from artificial intelligence-related listings, delivering standout returns as bankers line up a steady stream of summer debuts.

items.title: US Stock Futures Rise as Chipmaker Rebound Overshadows Iran War
items.description: US stock futures advanced on Tuesday as a rebound in chipmakers at the center of the artificial-intelligence trade overshadowed the re-escalation in the US-Iran war.

items.title: Saudi Crude Flow From Red Sea Hit Record Before Houthi Warning
items.description: Saudi Arabia exported record volumes of crude oil from terminals on the Red Sea in the days and weeks before the Houthis said they would block the nation’s shipments.

items.title: Chinese Oil Tanker U-Turns in Red Sea After Houthi Attack Threat
items.description: A very large crude carrier has made a U-turn in the southern Red Sea after warnings from Houthi rebels in Yemen that they would target ships heading to or from Saudi ports.

items.title: Economic Momentum Flips From Blessing to Curse for S&P 500 Bulls
items.description: Strong economic momentum is a double-edged sword for US equity traders. Solid growth is an earnings tailwind. But let it run too hot, and it turns from blessing to curse.

items.title: Shale Trades Go-Go Days of Growth for Output Creep
items.description: US drillers are letting production rise again — just not the way they used to.

items.title: Wall Street Talks Up Carry Trade as Returns Soar Most in Decades
items.description: One of the most enduring foreign-exchange bets is off to its best run in decades as surprisingly muted volatility across asset classes has investors piling into carry trades.

items.title: Victoria Debt Deal Faces Opposition From HSBC Bank
items.description: UK carpet maker Victoria Plc.’s bid to restructure its balance sheet is facing resistance from HSBC Holdings Plc, a holder of the company’s bonds.

items.title: Kazakh Oil Flows to Black Sea Forced to Stop as Tankers Hit
items.description: Kazakhstan is being forced to stop piping crude to its main export terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast as a spate of attacks on tankers jeopardizes the landlocked Central Asian country’s exports.

items.title: The Great British Takeover Tops $60 Billion After Mitie Deal
items.description: The UK stock market is losing the equivalent of more than $2 billion a week to takeovers of London-listed companies, with Mitie Group Plc the latest name to be snapped up.

items.title: Gold and Silver Rise on Dip-Buying as Traders Weigh Fed Strategy
items.description: Gold and silver rose on dip-buying, as traders monitored Middle East tensions for signs that higher energy costs could stoke inflation, putting pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten policy.

items.title: LA’s $1.2 Billion Graffiti Towers Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
items.description: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more t

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
items.description: Brent oil extended its recent rally as traders weighed threats to maritime traffic from the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea to a key Kazakh export terminal on Russia’s coast.

items.title: Stocks Rise as Rebound in Chipmakers Gathers Pace: Markets Wrap
items.description: Dip buyers extended a rebound in chipmakers, lifting global stock markets. Brent topped $90 a barrel as the US and Iran exchanged strikes for a 10th straight day.
